hobbies/Writing/chocolate-chip-cookies.title: My Favorite Chocolate Chip Cookies
hobbies/Writing/chocolate-chip-cookies.content:
The Best Brown Butter Chocolate Chip Cookies
This is my go-to cookie recipe. The secret is browning the butter until it develops a deep, toffee-like aroma, and letting the dough rest overnight in the fridge.
Ingredients

1 cup unsalted butter (browned)
1 cup dark brown sugar
½ cup granulated sugar
2 tsp vanilla extract
2 large eggs
2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
½ tsp sea salt
2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips/chunks

Instructions

Brown the butter: Melt the butter in a pan over medium heat. Keep swirling it until it foams and you see brown specks forming. Remove from heat immediately and let it cool for 15 minutes.
Mix the browned butter with the brown and white sugars.
Whisk in the eggs and vanilla until the mixture lightens.
Fold in the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, salt) just until combined.
Add the chocolate chips.
Important: Let the dough chill in the fridge overnight.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10-12 minutes.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt while hot!

Enjoy!

hobbies/book-club/manga--and--webtoons/i.title: Manga, Webtoons
hobbies/book-club/manga--and--webtoons/i.content:
I only started reading manga/webtoons after starting to watch anime. After a show’s season ends, I’m always too excited to wait for the next anime, so I jump to the manga to finish the story. And once I get hooked, I’m hooked, and will keep reading until the very end.
Summer 2024, I had jury duty, and while waiting in the lobby, I caught up on Lookism and read the entirety of My Hero Academia. Similarly, during my 2025 cruise to Mexico, on board the ship I read all of Naruto.
There’s a few series that I read weekly, otherwise I want to go through the “classics” and experience the joy of older work.

hobbies/the-tele/index.title: tv shows
hobbies/the-tele/index.content:
Growing up as a kid, I would watch cartoons on tv everyday in the morning. I grew up on Nickelodeon, Cartoon Network, and Disney Channel shows; Spongebob being my favorite amongst them.
Now that streaming exists, it feels like cable television is dying out. It’s so easy to just pick any episode and watch it from your phone, tablet, or computer from nearly anywhere in the world.
Streaming is super convenient, but it’s a bit sad in a way. I have fond memories of watching Survivor with my family at 8pm every Wednesday or The Amazing Race on Sundays. Sometimes, we’d have to set up the DVR to record the episode and then we’d watch it on the weekend so we wouldn’t be confused for the next episode.

index.title: Digital Garden
index.content:
Welcome to my Digital Garden! This is a place for me to write and share my thoughts, projects, and learnings with the world.
I really enjoy logging and keeping track of things, so in a way this garden will serve as a life journal for me. I hope to continuously add things over time.
Note: None of this is written with AI. Just pure human thoughts. Although you could probably already tell from my poor writing skills :D.

intellectual-greed/index.title: intellectual greed
intellectual-greed/index.content:
Knowledge = Rico
Knowledge is Power and Power is Money, so by commutative property Knowledge is Money.
Or rather, I could say I want to be rich with knowledge and hope that it helps me become money rich.
There’s an endless amount of learning in the world and in the modern day this knowledge is quite literally in the palm of your hand (or pocket).

academics
rabbit hole
